series_description:
Audits and review performed by or on
behalf of agency, including the working
papers. Also includes audits performed on
the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The Texas State Auditor's Office
(SAO) retains copies of its audits
performed on TX state agencies.

series_description:
Supporting documentation created and/or
used to legislative appropriation requests
by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending
required copies of the requests to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission
(TSLAC). Only copies of supporting
documentation submitted to the
Legislative Budget Board's (LBB) are
archival.

series_description:
Incoming outgoing and Internal
correspondence, in any format, pertaining
to the formulation, planning,
implementation, interpretation,
modification, or redefinition of the
programs, services, or projects of an
agency and the administrative regulations,
policies, and procedures that govern them.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
item number 1.1.008 should be
used only for correspondence that
is not included in or directly related
to another records series in this
schedule. For example, a
memorandum that documents an
appropriations request must be
retained for the minimum retention
period prescribed by item number
1.1.004; a letter concerning an
audit for that prescribed by item
number 1.1.002, etc. SEE ALSO

series_description:
Non-administrative incoming/outgoing
and internal correspondence, in any
media, pertaining to or arising from the
routine operations of the polices,
programs, services or projects of the
agency.
comments:
See comment to item number
1.1.007. SEE ALSO item number
1.1.010.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.

Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling or itinerary
records, purchased with state funds or
maintained by staff during business hours
that document appointments, itineraries
and other activities of agency officials or
employees.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected officials,
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when the records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: a record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state records
and subject to this retention period.
See Open Records Decision 635
issued in December 1995 by the
Atty. Gen.

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Atty.
Gen., including any requests eliciting the
opinions.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard
to pending litigation. SEE item
number 1.1.048.

series_description:
News, press releases, or any public
relations files maintained or issued by an
agency. Includes print, electronic, audio
and audiovisual records.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (CH. 552,
Gov't Code).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are exempted under the
Public Information Act (Ch.552, Gov't
Code).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency and adopted rules; open
meeting notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.

Surveys returned by the customer or client
of an agency, and the statistical data
maintained rating an agency's
performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
See item 1.1.067 4 summary
reports compiled from customer
surveys.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of the
agency in anticipation of or in adjudication
of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVE NOTE: Cases that set legal
precedents or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Service
Division of the TSLAC for archival
preservation.

series_description:
Information resources and operational
strategic plans prepared in accordance
with Sec. 2054.095 and 2056.002, Gov't
Code.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending
required copies of the plans to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission (TSLAC).

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non--
Fiscal) Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purposes of producing
non--fiscal reports.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data including in or
directly related to other record
series in this schedule. See
especially item 1.1.064.

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the Gov. and
legislature or as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending
required copies of the reports to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, TSLAC.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to the
executive and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contracts, and
similar activities.
comments:
CAUTION: SEE item number
1.1.064.

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies, and
Procedures - - Final
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public for those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
and agencies programs, services or
projects.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
SEE ALSO Agency Rules, Policies and
Procedures - Working Files 1.1.071.

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies, and
Procedures - - Working
Files
series_description:
Working Files used in development of
manuals, guidelines, administrative rules
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
and agencies programs, services or
projects.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments: SEE ALSO item number 1.1.070.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Agency Copy. Formally RMD 105. Includes
documentation of certification and
approval forms SLR 105C (formerly RMD
105C), and/or other forms designated by
the State Records Administration.
comments:
Original is retained by the State and
Local Records Management
Division, TSLAC.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.

Information in any format that is produced
by the authority of or at the total or partial
expense of a state agency or is required to
be distributed under law by the agency,
and is publicly distributed. The term does
not include information the distribution of
which is solely limited to contracts with or
grantees of the agency, staff persons
within the agency or within the
government agencies or members of the
public under the requests made under the
Public Information Act, Government Code
Chapter 552. The term includes but is not
limited to: a publication distributed in
print; on microfilm; as audiovisual
material; as interactive media or on
electronic storage device; as and on-line
publication; which is an index to other on-
line publication; as one or more text,
graphic, or other digital files; or as a user
interface to a computer database. A state
publications that is subject to a different,
longer retention. Should be kept until that
retention period ends. For example; items
1.1.004, 1.1.055, 1.1.066, 1.1.068, and
4.5.003 are state publications other the
definition, but are closely associated with
the appropriations process and must be
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, TSLAC, by law
(Government Code 441.101 -
441.106). The TSLAC will retain a
copy of all state publications
submitted to it on a continuing
basis, subject to periodic evaluation
to determine if the publication
merits further retention. For
additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the Internet, console
the administrative rules of the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program of the TSLAC (13 TAC 3.1 -
3.16).

series_description:
Background material, copy (drafts),
original artwork, photo negatives, prints,
flats, etc. This includes all work performed
both inside and outside the agency.

Automated software applications and
operating system files including job control
language, program listings/sores code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system
requirements/hardware configurations,
and equipment control systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records and their
timely, authorized disposition including
documentation describing how a system
operates and necessary for using the
system such as user guides, system or sub-
system definitions, system specifications,
input and output specifications, and
system flowcharts; program descriptions
and documentation such as program
flowcharts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, and other records that
document modifications to computer
programs; and data documentation
necessary to access, retrieved, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated system
such as a data element dictionary, file
layout, codebook or table or other records
that explain the meeting, purpose,
structure, logical relationships, and origin
of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties and License
Agreement.

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.

Application, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisory level except in those agencies
would counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection by employees of life,
disability, health, and other types of
insurance offered by the State of Texas to
its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

Includes notes of interviews with
candidates, questions asked of applicants,
audio and videotapes of job interviews,
driving record and previous injury checks,
pre--employment physical exams, pre-
employment drug screening test results,
and all other records that document that
selection process.
comments:
CAUTION: does not include criminal
history checks. SEE item number
3.1.026.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the
best interests of the state, the agency, or
the employee workforce; and for failure to
improve performance or conduct following
imposition of corrective action. They
include cause for demotion, suspension, or
dismissal, and the reasons for failure to
give written notice of resignation. They
also be used to document evidence of
employee self-improvement efforts, as
well as favorable and unfavorable
communications.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the
records of an employee including
paygrade, position classification, employee
number, evaluation date, and termination
of employment.

Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain criminal
history records information from
DPS must refer to its agencies
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, transcripts, test
scores, or similar records documenting the
training, testing, or continuing education
achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Employment Eligibility,
Document or Verification
of
series_description: Federal Reporting Form (INS I-9)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS I-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, which ever is later.
Agencies should make certain that
the INS I-9 forms for employees
who terminate from an agency less
than 3 years from date of hire are
kept for 3 years retention period. 8
CFR 274a.2(b)(2)(i)(A) and (c)(2).

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection of available benefit options
other than insurance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_description:
Retention period applies if an agency
applies to the sender of a resume that it
will be kept on file should future job
openings occur.
comments:
See item number 3.1.014 4
resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any
way in the employment selection
process.

series_description:
Form completed and signed by employee
or official, or former employee or official,
electing to keep home address, home
telephone number, social security number,
and family information open or
confidential under the Public Information
Act, Government Code 552.024.
comments: SEE item number 3.3.011.

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court-ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employee's gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.

Employer's copy of "Employee's
Withholding Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
For instructions regarding the

accounts and for additional

period see the most current edition
of the "Benefits Coordinator
Reference Manual" issued by the
Employee Retirement System of
Texas.

series_description: Includes supporting documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Minimum information needed to verify
employment, includes name, Social
Security number, exact dates of
employment, last known address and
most recent public access option form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years
comments: SEE item number 3.1.038.

series_title:
Reimbursable Activities,
Requests and
Authorizations to Engage
In
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimburse.

Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agency-wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of the agency.

Includes documentation used to complete
EEO reports.

Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle
and provides employee leave status for
each position.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick Leave
Requests

series_description:
Requests submitted, approvals, number of
hours transferred in and out, etc.

comments:
CAUTION: Does not include long-
distance telephone billing detail.
SEE item numbers 5.5.001, 5.5.006,
and 5.5.007.

series_description:
Production, job, labor, quotes, pricing,
specifications, etc.
Transfer or adjustments to budget.
Direct Deposit Registers.
Cash deposit slips.

Includes receipts for fees (permits,
licenses, renewals, etc.).

Orders, statements, and change orders,
etc.

Requisitions, orders, receiving reports,
invoices, or statements, change orders,
best value determinations, etc.
Travel, payroll, etc.
Includes agency monthly budget reports.

Required By the General Appropriations
Act (100 Day Report).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
CAUTION: The archival
requirement, when biannual or
annual narrative report is not
produced, is met by sending
required copies of the reports to
the Texas State Publications
Depository, TSLAC.

series_description:
Required by the General Appropriations
Act.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
accepted bids, performance bonds,
contracts, purchase orders, inspection
reports, and correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Any mailing address, telephone or fax
number, or email address records
maintained by an agency on its employees
or on entities or persons it serves.

Records and reports of postage expenses,
including postal meter usage.

Schedules of prices charged by an agency
for services to the public or other
agencies, including any documentation
used to determine the charges.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
Agreements or contracts between the
agency and equipment vendor to provide
maintenance service for equipment.
Includes requests for installation, movies,
service, etc.; and service/repair logbooks,
etc.

Updates agency portion of the inventory
listing and adds, changes, transfers, or
deletes items from the inventory.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

Estimate Files (Supply and
Repair Cost Estimates).

series_description: Property, equipment, supply verification.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

Fire, safety, and other inspection records

inspection records of building
construction.

series_description:
Logs, registers, or similar records
documenting visitors to limited access or
restricted areas of agency facilities.

Records relating to the issuance of keys, ID

series_description:
In addition to summary detail, includes

AN billing detail.

series_description:
In addition to summary detail, includes
any accompanying detailed listing of long-

Documentation relating to disputed long-

Charge or bill statements received by

Records of training given employees in an

comments:
Texas Health and Safety Code,
502.009(g).

series_description:
Records relating to the review of
employee grievances against personnel

comments:
